title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concluded his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Bruins plan to bolster their lineup by possibly signing Hagens to an entry-level contract soon.
Hagens, chosen seventh in the 2025 NHL Draft, is expected to be a future top-six player for Boston.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?', available on Disney+, about a man turning to stand-up comedy.
Chuck Norris stars in 1983's 'Lone Wolf McQuade', streaming on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man', featuring Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan, set during WWII, streams on Netflix.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora's current contract with the Red Sox runs through the 2027 season, earning over $7 million annually.
Cora is set to surpass Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager, needing 134 more victories.
Cora emphasized family considerations in his decision-making, particularly his children's education as they grow older.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line, featuring Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt, has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
Boston's second line boasts a goal differential of +19, second only to the Colorado Avalanche's top line in the NHL.
Zacha leads the line with career-best performance, scoring 23 goals and 51 points in 66 games so far this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric cancer care at MGH.
Mahr's participation is inspired by young patients he witnessed on the course in 2014, emphasizing their courage.
Pediatric cancer research receives limited funding, motivating Mahr to raise awareness and support for affected families.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts income tax proposal aims to reduce rate from 5% to 4%, saving median households about $1,250 annually.
Tufts analysis suggests proposed tax cut would decrease state revenue by approximately $5.1 billion annually, a 10% decline.
A 78% majority of Boston.com poll participants favor tax cut, with opinions divided on impact to state-funded programs.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church stands trial for allegedly murdering boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in a suspected love triangle dispute.
Prosecutors claim Church poisoned Fowler's drink with ethylene glycol, citing her history of threatening remarks.
Church's defense argues her actions were misinterpreted, and Fowler died from substance abuse, not murder.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes legislation to lift Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban, supported by MassWildlife.
The proposal includes allowing all hunters to use crossbows and reducing setbacks for bowhunters near dwellings.
Massachusetts has high deer population densities, with some areas exceeding 30 deer per square mile.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a derringer pistol in a case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The pistol, remarkably well-preserved, has drawn significant online attention with over 1.2 million subreddit views.
DeMontigny immediately contacted police after the discovery, as required for non-antique weapons he finds.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old man, Sergio Josue Castellanos, is charged with murder after a Chelsea stabbing incident.
The stabbing occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, leaving one victim dead after surgery.
Chelsea police found several victims, but have not disclosed their identities; the investigation continues.
